title: has_more_changes fallback to full reload: required JMAP spec behavior, not an optimization
description: In chat_delta_sync, when the server returns has_more_changes=true, the code discards the partial delta and falls back to a full Chat/query + Chat/get reload. This is mandatory JMAP behavior per RFC 8620 §5.5: when a /changes response sets hasMoreChanges, the client cannot apply the partial delta (it lacks a consistent new_state to advance to) and must re-fetch the full set. The 500-item maxChanges limit means large bulk imports or server-side migrations can trigger this. A future contributor might remove the fallback thinking 'we already got the created/updated ids, just apply them' — but that would leave chat_state pointing at a stale baseline, causing all subsequent delta syncs to request changes from an already-consumed state token, either getting empty results or a cannotCalculateChanges error.
